👀 Jack Dorsey just launched Buzz, an open source alternative to Slack built for humans and AI agents

The Twitter cofounder’s company, Block, has released a new collaboration platform where AI agents aren’t treated like add ons. They’re part of the team.

Think Slack, but designed for an agentic workplace from the start.

What that means in practice:

• AI agents get their own accounts and permissions
• They can join conversations, send messages, write code, and review it
• Teams can manage GitHub projects without leaving Buzz
• You still get the familiar basics: channels, threads, DMs, voice chat, and media sharing

What makes Buzz more interesting is what’s happening underneath.

It’s open source, decentralized, and model agnostic, so teams aren’t locked into a specific AI provider. It runs on the Nostr protocol, with messages, code changes, and other workflow activity recorded in a shared event log.

Companies can also self host the entire platform and customize it around their own workflows.

Dorsey says part of the goal is to reduce reliance on Slack and GitHub.

But the bigger idea here is probably more important:

Instead of squeezing AI agents into software built for humans, Buzz is building the workspace around humans and agents working side by side.

It’s available now on macOS, Windows, and Linux. The project is still early, and there’s no mobile app yet.

https://buzz.xyz/

🙃 Elon Musk is extremely unhappy with Nolan’s “Odyssey”, he wants to make his own AI version

Elon Musk said that his AI model Grok Imagine will create a full-length film adaptation of “The Odyssey” by the end of 2026.

Musk made the statement after the release of Christopher Nolan’s adaptation of “The Odyssey”, which sparked heated debates among viewers and critics.

The film, starring Matt Damon as Odysseus, received mixed reactions: some viewers criticized the modern interpretation of the ancient epic, the casting choices, and the departure from Greek traditions.

Social media users also actively discussed the film’s visual style, costumes, and the director’s creative decisions, including the casting and reinterpretation of ancient Greek heroes.

In Greece, the film was met with a very cold reaction. Viewers and politicians are asking: why are there almost no Greek actors in a story about ancient Greece?

If Musk’s project is completed, it could become one of the first attempts to create a full-length movie using generative AI.

@aipost 🏴

❗️Uber just admitted what many companies have been hinting at.

The company has cut 10% of its customer service workforce, saying the move is part of simplifying operations and embracing AI.

What’s notable is that this is the first time Uber has directly linked layoffs to automation.

According to Megha Yethadka, who leads Uber’s global community operations, the team’s biggest problem wasn’t just staffing, it was complexity. She told employees the organization had become too fragmented and siloed, making it difficult for AI to deliver meaningful improvements.

Her message was simple: before AI can replace or enhance work at scale, companies need to redesign how that work is organized.

🇺🇸 Anthropic donates $40M to a US political group ahead of the midterm elections.

The AI firm doubled its previous $20M commitment to Public First Action, which campaigns for stronger AI safeguards.

Anthropic says the donation was made to support Public First’s AI policy mission, not election campaigns.

@aipost 🏴
